stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • 1872: The US Congress designated Yellowstone as the first national park in the United States.
  • 1890: Congress established Yosemite National Park, transferring federal stewardship to the US Army.
  • 1916: Congress created the National Park Service (NPS), giving it authority over Yellowstone, Yosemite, and other federal parklands.
  • The NPS’s first director, Stephen Mather, expanded park infrastructure and visitor services.
  • The NPS remains the federal agency responsible for the national parks.
stem: The student wants to specify the order in which different federal entities were given the authority to manage US national parks. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Authority over US national parks shifted from Congress’s direct designation to the US Army in 1890, then from the US Army to the National Park Service in 1916.
Congress, the US Army, and the National Park Service all had authority over US national parks, but they possessed this authority at different times.
The National Park Service has managed US national parks since it was established by Congress in 1916.
The US Army managed Yosemite from 1890 until 1916, at which point authority was transferred to Yellowstone by Stephen Mather.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence specifies the order in which different federal entities were given the authority to manage US national parks, noting that this role shifted from direct congressional designation to the US Army in 1890, then from the US Army to the National Park Service in 1916.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence mentions that all three entities had authority at different times, it doesn’t specify the chronological order. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence only mentions when the NPS began managing the parks; it doesn’t specify the order in which different entities held that authority. Choice D is incorrect because it misrepresents information from the notes; authority was transferred to the NPS in 1916, not to Yellowstone, and Mather’s role is misrepresented.
